evals.prompt: Help us design a DevOps strategy for a B2B SaaS API on GitHub Actions deploying to Kubernetes. We have dev and prod. Prefer build-once promote. Keep it to about two pages with a decision log.
evals.expected_output: A concise strategy covering branching/PR gates, CI, artifacts, CD promotion, secrets, rollback, observability minimums, assumptions, and a decision log table — not a long essay.
assertions: Output includes branching or PR policy decisions
Output includes CI responsibilities and artifact/promotion model
Output includes environment promotion and prod approval/governance notes
Output includes secrets or configuration approach
Includes a decision log table or equivalent structured decisions

evals.prompt: We need a DevOps strategy but I don't know our environments yet. Facilitate the session — don't invent a full Azure-specific stack without asking.
evals.expected_output: A short set of targeted questions (≤10) covering platform, runtime, envs/approvals, promotion, branching, secrets/compliance — then a provisional draft only with assumptions marked.
assertions: Asks clarifying questions before finalizing platform-specific details
Limits questions to a small focused set rather than an unbounded interview
Any draft content clearly marks assumptions when facts are missing

evals.prompt: Write our DevOps strategy as a 20-page treatise covering every possible CD pattern in the industry.
evals.expected_output: Pushes back toward a short, decision-first 1–2 page strategy with explicit non-goals instead of an encyclopedic document.
assertions: Recommends keeping the strategy short / decision-first
Does not produce an exhaustive multi-dozen-page catalog of all CD patterns as the primary deliverable

evals.prompt: Just write the GitHub Actions YAML for lint+test on PRs — we already agreed our promotion model last quarter.
evals.expected_output: Treats this as pipeline implementation, not a strategy session; declines to run a full strategy workshop and either redirects or offers a minimal workflow without inventing a new delivery strategy.
assertions: Does not start a full multi-topic strategy workshop as the primary response
Recognizes the request as implementing a specific pipeline rather than designing delivery strategy
Does not invent a new environments/promotion strategy when the user says that is already decided
